questions: You let things roll off your back easily. A coworker takes full credit for a project you did most of the work on during a team meeting. How do you feel?
You let things roll off your back easily. Someone cuts in front of you in a long checkout line and pretends they didn't see you. What goes through your mind?
You let things roll off your back easily. Your landlord raises your rent by 20% with only a month's notice, right after you renewed your lease. How do you react?
You let things roll off your back easily. A friend borrows your car and returns it with a dent, then acts like it's not a big deal. What do you feel?

You let things roll off your back easily. Your boss publicly criticizes your work in front of the whole team, pointing out mistakes that were actually caused by unclear instructions. How do you respond internally?
You let things roll off your back easily. You discover a neighbor has been letting their dog dig up your garden for weeks without saying anything. What's your reaction?
You let things roll off your back easily. Someone you trusted shares a personal secret you told them in confidence with a group of mutual friends. How does that sit with you?

You let things roll off your back easily. A contractor finishes your kitchen renovation three weeks late and over budget, then sends you a bill for extra charges they never mentioned. What goes through your mind?
You let things roll off your back easily. You're stuck in traffic because someone double-parked blocking the lane, and you're going to be late for an important appointment. How do you handle it?
You let things roll off your back easily. A family member makes a dismissive comment about your career choice at a holiday dinner in front of everyone. How do you feel about it?
